subject: Public Health
title: Case-Control Studies: Looking Backward
description: A 60-second lesson on Case-Control Studies: Looking Backward.

hook.text: Start with people who are already sick, then ask what they were exposed to—that's the case-control study's logic.

buildup.text: Cases are people with the disease. Controls are similar people without it. Compare their past exposures.

discovery.text: Case-control studies are ideal for rare diseases—you don't need thousands of people to find enough cases.

twist.text: The link between thalidomide and birth defects was discovered through a case-control study in just weeks.

climax.text: Selection of appropriate controls is critical—biased controls can produce misleading results.

punchline.text: Can't follow thousands forward? Start with the sick and work backward.

quiz.question: When are case-control studies most useful?
options: When a quick treatment is needed
